global.title: Powerhouse uses GraphQL schemas as a lingua franca between stakeholders and pairs naturally with CQRS separating queries, mutations, and subscriptions
global.content: Powerhouse chose GraphQL schemas as the common language (lingua franca) bridging designers, developers, and non-technical stakeholders. Schemas provide a structured representation of data types, relationships, and operations accessible as a single source of truth. For non-technical contributors, schemas offer a readable map of data flows; for developers, they serve as implementation contracts. GraphQL pairs naturally with CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation): queries retrieve data, mutations handle modifications, subscriptions enable real-time updates. Combined with TypeScript, auto-generated types ensure consistency between client and server, eliminating runtime errors. This approach enables Rapid Application Development (RAD) by allowing front-end developers to plug into any document model without managing backend or state management.
global.description: GraphQL schemas serve as single source of truth readable by both technical and non-technical stakeholders, aligning with CQRS patterns.
